summary: Implemented PR Tracker service (pr-tracker.ts) and Exercise History service (exercise-history.ts). PR Tracker detects weight and volume PRs, supports recalculation for rollback on workout deletion, and provides Epley 1RM estimation. Exercise History provides recent session summaries, exercise detail summaries with PRs, and total session counts.
keyDecisions: PR types: 'weight' (max single-set weight) and 'volume' (max weight*reps per set) tracked independently
checkAndRecordPR returns the first PR type detected (weight checked before volume), but both are recorded in DB if both thresholds exceeded
recalculatePR deletes all existing PRs for an exercise then rescans all workout_sets via workout_exercises to rebuild from scratch
getEstimated1RM uses Epley formula: weight * (1 + reps/30)
Exercise History uses per-WorkoutExercise grouping to handle same-exercise-multiple-times (US-17)
Skipped exercises are excluded from both PR calculations and session counts
Used type aliases instead of empty interfaces for service type exports to satisfy @typescript-eslint/no-empty-object-type

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: checkAndRecordPR detects new PR and creates PersonalRecord
PR types: weight (max single-set weight) and volume (max weight*reps)
recalculatePR scans all WorkoutSets and rebuilds PR records from scratch
getPRList() returns all PR records grouped by exercise
getExerciseSummary returns ExerciseDetailSummary with recent 5 sessions + PRs + total count
getRecentSessions returns session summaries with set details
Deleting a workout with PR triggers recalculatePR and PR rolls back to previous max
Unit tests: PR detection, rollback, 1RM estimation, session aggregation

notes: All 362 project tests pass (including 45 new tests). PR tracker coverage: 96.77% statements. Exercise history coverage: 97.05% statements. Services use existing repository pattern with sql.js in-memory testing.
